stories.title: Schwarber Hits 19th and 20th HRs — Seven Straight, Goes for Record Tonight
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Schwarber streak covered May 13-15 at 5-6 consecutive games). Last night (May 15) vs Pittsburgh: 2 HRs (19th and 20th), Phillies 11-9 in 10 innings. Now at 7 consecutive games with an HR. Tonight: game 8 would TIE the all-time MLB record held by Don Mattingly (Yankees, 1987), Ken Griffey Jr. (Mariners, 1993), and Dale Long (Pirates, 1956). Mattingly is his own manager. First player to reach 20 HRs in 45 games. On pace for 67-72 HRs.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber homered TWICE last night (No. 19 and 20).

That's 7 consecutive games with a HR.

Tonight, game 8 would TIE the all-time MLB record — held by Mattingly, Griffey Jr., and Dale Long.

Also: Don Mattingly is his manager. 😤

#Phillies #Schwarber #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: 20 HRs in 45 games.

He's the FIRST player to reach 20 this season.

He's also one of the only Phillies in history to hit 20 before May 16.

Cy Williams did it in 1923. That's the company we're talking about.

Game 8 tonight. Record on the line. 🔥

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Hits Nos. 19 and 20, Extends Streak to 7 Straight — Tonight He Can Tie the MLB Record

**Freshness:** Game played May 15, fresh for May 16 content. Record-tying attempt tonight is highly time-sensitive.

stories.title: Schlittler Dominates Subway Series; Soto Hits 250th Career HR in Loss
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Rivalry Weekend preview covered May 15). Yankees 5-2 Mets. Cam Schlittler (25): 6.2 IP, 1 H, 9 K, 2 BB — AL-best ERA held at 1.35. Juan Soto's 250th career HR in the 7th (0-2 fastball, 393 ft, 102.6 mph) was the only real threat. Jazz Chisholm 2-run double in 3-run 3rd off Clay Holmes. Ben Rice 14th HR. At 27, Soto joins 250 HR/95 SB before age 28 with A-Rod, Griffey, Mantle, Trout, Frank Robinson, Andruw Jones.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Cam Schlittler: 6.2 IP. 1 hit. 9 K. ERA 1.35 (AL best).

In the Subway Series. At 25 years old.

Juan Soto hit his 250th career HR in the 7th — 393 ft, 102.6 mph, off an 0-2 pitch.

Yankees 5, Mets 2. ⚾

#Yankees #Mets #SubwaySeries

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Juan Soto hit his 250th career home run last night. He's 27.

At 27 with 250+ HRs and 95+ steals, he joins: A-Rod, Griffey Jr., Mantle, Mike Trout, Frank Robinson, and Andruw Jones.

That's it. That's the whole list.

#Mets #JuanSoto #MLB

stories.title: Cubs 10-5 White Sox — Carson Kelly Sparks Crosstown Classic Opener

stories.title: Oneil Cruz: 10 HR, 15 SB in 39 Games — Historic Power-Speed Start
stories.angle: NEW STORY. Pittsburgh Pirates SS Oneil Cruz has 10 HRs and 15 SBs through his 39th game, making him only the 3rd player in MLB history to reach those marks before their 40th game, joining Eric Davis (1987) and Lou Brock (1967). Historic pace for the 6-foot-7 Pirates shortstop.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Oneil Cruz: 10 HRs and 15 steals in his first 39 games.

Only two players in MLB history did this before their 40th game: Eric Davis (1987) and Lou Brock (1967).

The 6'7" Pirates shortstop is quietly writing history.

#Pirates #OncilCruz #MLB

**Confidence Notes:**
- The exact number of games (39) is from the week ending May 14 data. By May 16, Cruz may have played 40-41 games. Use "entering this week" phrasing rather than "entering Saturday exactly at 39."
- Eric Davis (1987) and Lou Brock (1967) comparisons — HIGH confidence (from MLB source)
**Freshness:** Milestone from week of May 11-14, relatively fresh for May 16 analysis piece.

stories.title: Braves 3-2 Red Sox; Matt Olson's Streak Reaches 826 Games
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (NL standings/Braves covered May 15). Braves topped Red Sox 3-2 Friday, maintaining NL-best form. Matt Olson's consecutive games streak reached 826, making it the 10th-longest in MLB history, dating to May 2, 2021. NL East leaders entering Saturday with Phillies surging and Cubs in the mix.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Atlanta beats Boston 3-2. Routine Friday for the Braves.

Not so routine: Matt Olson has now played 826+ consecutive games. That's the 10th-longest streak in MLB history, dating back to May 2021.

The most consistent player you're not talking about. 🎯

#Braves #MattOlson #MLB
